key_insight: Defect-free FeS2(100) terraces can host two self-organized L-cysteine supramolecular phases, showing that pristine pyrite surfaces—not just defects—can drive prebiotic molecular assembly.
engineering_relevance: This suggests surface crystallinity and electronic structure are critical design variables for catalytic/mineral interfaces intended to promote controlled self-assembly and polymerization.
method: STM/STS to image and probe L-cysteine adsorption on pyrite terraces
DFT calculations to identify intermolecular and substrate-driven stabilization
Comparison of two supramolecular phases: compact ordered vs. less packed labile networks
evidence_signal: The abstract reports atomically ordered, defect-free FeS2 terraces and identifies trimer-based interactions consistent with the observed STM patterns, supported by DFT.
watch_item: The assemblies were observed under specific surface-preparation and UHV-style characterization conditions, so extending the mechanism to realistic prebiotic environments requires caution.
paper_title: Prebiotic Chemistry Assemblies of L-Cysteine on Defect-Free Pyrite Terraces
